steps.title: Test the hypothesis
steps.content: The hypothesis predicts that, in low visibility with medium-sized vortices, wider-headed fish should be worse than narrower-headed fish at detecting obstructions. The support must match those conditions.

steps.title: Require a direct comparison
steps.content: Choice A compares only specimens of one narrow-headed species. Choice C shows equal performance, not worse performance for wider-headed fish. Choice D gives only the wide-headed bristlemouth's result without a narrow-headed comparison. The remaining choice compares both fish under the right conditions and shows the wider-headed fish bumping into more obstructions. The answer is B.
